RBI/FMRD/2025-26/142
FMRD.DIRD.04/14.03.038/2025-26
November 11, 2025
To
All participants in repo market
Dear Sir/Madam
Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Repurchase Transactions (Repo))
Directions, 2025
Please refer to the Repurchase Transactions (Repo) (Reserve Bank) Directions,
2018 dated July 24, 2018, as amended from time to time.

logical_units.title: 2. The Central Government in exercise of the powers conferred by clause (e) of
logical_units.text:
2. The Central Government in exercise of the powers conferred by clause (e) of
section 45U of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 (2 of 1934), has specified the
Municipal Debt Securities, having the meaning assigned to it in the Securities and
Exchange Board of India Act, 1992 (15 of 1992) or the rules or regulations made
thereunder, to be as security under the said section for the purposes of "repo" and
"reverse repo" vide notification dated October 22, 2025 in the Official Gazette.

logical_units.title: 3. Accordingly, the aforesaid Directions are being updated to include Municipal Debt
logical_units.text:
3. Accordingly, the aforesaid Directions are being updated to include Municipal Debt
Securities as eligible securities for repo transactions. The Master Direction - Reserve
Bank of India (Repurchase Transactions (Repo)) Directions, 2025, have been issued
today and are enclosed herewith.

logical_units.title: 4. These Directions have been issued by the Reserve Bank in exercise of the powers
logical_units.text:
4. These Directions have been issued by the Reserve Bank in exercise of the powers
conferred under section 45W of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, read with
section 45U of the Act and of all the powers enabling it in this behalf.

logical_units.title: 5. These Directions shall be applicable with immediate effect.
logical_units.text:
5. These Directions shall be applicable with immediate effect.
Yours faithfully
(Dimple Bhandia)
Chief General Manager
RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
FINANCIAL MARKETS REGULATION DEPARTMENT
9th FLOOR, CENTRAL OFFICE BUILDING, FORT
MUMBAI 400 001
Notification No. FMRD.DIRD.05/14.03.038/2025-26 dated Nov 11, 2025
Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Repurchase Transactions (Repo))
Directions, 2025
In exercise of the powers conferred by section 45W of the Reserve Bank of India Act,
1934 (RBI Act) and of all the powers enabling it in this behalf, the Reserve Bank of
India (the Reserve Bank) having considered it necessary in public interest and with
a view to regulate the financial system of the country to its advantage, hereby issues
the following directions to all the persons eligible to participate or transact business
in market repurchase transactions (repos) in India.

logical_units.title: 1. Short title, commencement and applicability of the directions
logical_units.text:
1. Short title, commencement and applicability of the directions
(1) These Directions shall be called as the Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India
(Repurchase Transactions (Repo)) Directions, 2025 and shall supersede all other
directions issued on the subject and covered by these regulations. These Directions
shall come into force with immediate effect.
(2) These Directions shall be applicable to repurchase transactions (Repo),
undertaken on recognized stock exchanges, electronic trading platforms (ETP) and
Over-the-Counter (OTC) to the extent stated herein. In case of exchange traded
repurchase transactions (Repo), procedure for execution and settlement of trades
shall be in accordance with the rules and regulations issued by the recognized stock
exchange/Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI).
(3) These Directions shall not apply to repo/ reverse repo transactions under the
Liquidity Adjustment Facility and the Marginal Standing Facility, which would
continue to be regulated as per the existing regulations.

logical_units.title: 2. Definitions
logical_units.text:
2. Definitions
(1) In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires-
Explanation: A 'repo' transaction by an entity is 'reverse repo' transaction for the
counterpart entity. For the purpose of these Directions, the word 'repo' is used to
mean both 'repo' and 'reverse repo' with the appropriate meaning applied
contextually.

logical_units.title: (a) "Corporate bonds and debentures" mean non-convertible debt securities
logical_units.text:
(a) "Corporate bonds and debentures" mean non-convertible debt securities
issued in India which create or acknowledge indebtedness, including (i) debentures

logical_units.title: (ii) bonds (iii) commercial papers (iv) certificate of deposits and such other securities
logical_units.text:
(ii) bonds (iii) commercial papers (iv) certificate of deposits and such other securities
of a company, a multilateral financial institution (MFI) or a body corporate constituted
by or under a Central Act or a State Act, whether constituting a charge on the assets
of the company or body corporate or not, but does not include debt securities issued
by Central Government or a State Government, or such other persons as may be
specified by the Reserve Bank, security receipts and securitized debt instruments.

logical_units.title: (b) "Commercial Paper (CP)" is an unsecured money market instrument issued in
logical_units.text:
(b) "Commercial Paper (CP)" is an unsecured money market instrument issued in
the form of a promissory note. The original tenor of a CP shall be between seven
days to one year.

logical_units.title: (c) "Certificate of Deposit (CD)" is a negotiable money market instrument and
logical_units.text:
(c) "Certificate of Deposit (CD)" is a negotiable money market instrument and
issued in dematerialized form or as a Usance Promissory Note against funds
deposited at a bank or other eligible financial institution for a specified time period.

logical_units.title: (d) "Debt ETF" is an exchange traded fund that invests only in eligible securities
logical_units.text:
(d) "Debt ETF" is an exchange traded fund that invests only in eligible securities
specified in para 3(1) of these directions.

logical_units.title: (e) "Delivery versus Payment (DvP)" is a settlement mechanism which stipulates
logical_units.text:
(e) "Delivery versus Payment (DvP)" is a settlement mechanism which stipulates
that transfer of funds from the buyer of securities is made simultaneously with the
transfer of securities by the seller of securities.

logical_units.title: (f) "Government securities" shall have the same meaning as defined in Section
logical_units.text:
(f) "Government securities" shall have the same meaning as defined in Section
2(f) of the Government Securities Act, 2006.

logical_units.title: (g) "Haircut" is the difference between the market value of the collateral and the
logical_units.text:
(g) "Haircut" is the difference between the market value of the collateral and the
amount borrowed/lent against that collateral.

logical_units.title: (h) "Listed corporate" means a company or firm whose shares and (or) debt are
logical_units.text:
(h) "Listed corporate" means a company or firm whose shares and (or) debt are
listed and traded on a recognized stock exchange/s.

logical_units.title: (i) "MFIs" are multilateral financial institutions in which Government of India is a
logical_units.text:
(i) "MFIs" are multilateral financial institutions in which Government of India is a
member.

logical_units.title: (j) "Municipal Debt Securities" shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in
logical_units.text:
(j) "Municipal Debt Securities" shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in
the Securities and Exchange Board of India Act, 1992 (15 of 1992) or the rules or
regulations made thereunder.

logical_units.title: (k) "Recognized stock exchange" shall have the same meaning as defined in
logical_units.text:
(k) "Recognized stock exchange" shall have the same meaning as defined in
Section 2 (f) of Securities Contracts (Regulation) Act, 1956 (42 of 1956).

logical_units.title: (l) "Regulated entity" means any person, other than an individual or HUF, whose
logical_units.text:
(l) "Regulated entity" means any person, other than an individual or HUF, whose
business activities are being regulated by any one of the financial regulators in India
viz., Reserve Bank of India, Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI),
Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI), Pension Fund
Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA), National Housing Bank (NHB) and
National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD).

logical_units.title: (m) "Related entity" of a company or a firm means any company or firm which is (i)
logical_units.text:
(m) "Related entity" of a company or a firm means any company or firm which is (i)
a holding, subsidiary or an associate company of such company; or (ii) a subsidiary
of a holding company to which it is also a subsidiary. The holding, subsidiary and
associate company shall have the same meaning as defined in Companies Act,
2013.

logical_units.title: (n) "Repo" shall have the same meaning as defined in Section 45U (c) of RBI Act,
logical_units.text:
(n) "Repo" shall have the same meaning as defined in Section 45U (c) of RBI Act,
1934.
"Reverse Repo" shall have the same meaning as defined in Section 45U (d) of RBI
Act, 1934.

logical_units.title: 3. Eligible securities for repo
logical_units.text:
3. Eligible securities for repo
(1) The securities eligible for repo under these Directions shall include:

logical_units.title: (a) Government securities issued by the Central Government or a State Government.
logical_units.text: (a) Government securities issued by the Central Government or a State Government.

logical_units.title: (b) Listed corporate bonds and debentures, subject to the condition that no
logical_units.text:
(b) Listed corporate bonds and debentures, subject to the condition that no
participant shall borrow against the collateral of its own securities, or securities
issued by a related entity.

logical_units.title: (c) Commercial Papers (CPs) and Certificate of Deposits (CDs).
logical_units.text: (c) Commercial Papers (CPs) and Certificate of Deposits (CDs).

logical_units.title: (d) Units of Debt ETFs.
logical_units.text: (d) Units of Debt ETFs.

logical_units.title: (e) Municipal Debt Securities.
logical_units.text: (e) Municipal Debt Securities.

logical_units.title: (f) Any other security of a local authority as may be specified in this behalf by the
logical_units.text:
(f) Any other security of a local authority as may be specified in this behalf by the
Central Government.

logical_units.title: 4. Eligible participants
logical_units.text:
4. Eligible participants
(1) The following are eligible to participate in repo transaction under these Directions:

logical_units.title: (a) Any regulated entity.
logical_units.text: (a) Any regulated entity.

logical_units.title: (b) Any listed corporate.
logical_units.text: (b) Any listed corporate.

logical_units.title: (c) Any unlisted company, which has been issued special securities by the
logical_units.text:
(c) Any unlisted company, which has been issued special securities by the
Government of India, using only such special securities as collateral.

logical_units.title: (d) Any All India Financial Institution (FIs) viz. Exim Bank, NABARD, NHB, Small
logical_units.text:
(d) Any All India Financial Institution (FIs) viz. Exim Bank, NABARD, NHB, Small
Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI) and National Bank for Financing
Infrastructure and Development, constituted by an Act of Parliament and

logical_units.title: (e) Any other entity approved by the Reserve Bank from time to time for this purpose.
logical_units.text: (e) Any other entity approved by the Reserve Bank from time to time for this purpose.

logical_units.title: 5. Tenor
logical_units.text:
5. Tenor
Repos shall be undertaken for a minimum period of one day and a maximum period
of one year.

logical_units.title: 6. Tri-Party Agent
logical_units.text:
6. Tri-Party Agent
The eligibility criteria, roles and obligation, application procedure for authorization,
and exit procedure for Tri-party Agent are given in Annex I of these Directions.

logical_units.title: 7. Trading venues
logical_units.text:
7. Trading venues
Repo transactions may be traded on any recognized stock exchanges, or an
electronic trading platform (ETP) duly authorised by the Reserve Bank or in the over-
the-counter (OTC) market. However, prior approval of the Reserve Bank is required
for trading repos on any trading platform, including on recognized stock exchanges.

logical_units.title: 8. Trading process
logical_units.text:
8. Trading process
Repo transactions, including tri-party repo transactions, may use any mutually
agreed trading process, including but not limited to, bilateral or multilateral, quote
driven or order driven processes, anonymous or otherwise.

logical_units.title: 9. Reporting of trades
logical_units.text:
9. Reporting of trades
(1) All repo transactions, other than those on recognized stock exchanges or on
approved electronic trading platforms, that disseminate trade information on the
platforms, shall be reported within 15 minutes of the trade: repo in Government
securities to the Clearcorp Repo Order Matching System (CROMS) and repo in other
eligible securities to the reporting platform F-TRAC, respectively.
(2) All trading and reporting platforms, including recognized stock exchanges, for
repo transactions shall provide any data or other information to the Reserve Bank or
to any entity as may be required by the Reserve Bank.
(3) The participants to repo transactions acting under these Directions shall furnish
any information or data sought by the Reserve Bank within the period stipulated in
the letter/mail issued to the participant to furnish such information or data.

logical_units.title: 10. Settlement of trades
logical_units.text:
10. Settlement of trades
(1) Settlement of trades under these Directions shall be-

logical_units.title: (a) The first leg of all repo transactions shall settle either on a T+0 or T+1 basis.
logical_units.text: (a) The first leg of all repo transactions shall settle either on a T+0 or T+1 basis.

logical_units.title: (b) All repo transactions shall settle on a Delivery vs Payments (DvP) basis.
logical_units.text: (b) All repo transactions shall settle on a Delivery vs Payments (DvP) basis.

logical_units.title: (c) All repos in government securities shall settle through CCIL or any other clearing
logical_units.text:
(c) All repos in government securities shall settle through CCIL or any other clearing
agency approved by the Reserve Bank.

logical_units.title: (d) All repos in other eligible securities shall settle through the clearing house of
logical_units.text:
(d) All repos in other eligible securities shall settle through the clearing house of
exchanges or any other entity which has been approved by the Reserve Bank.

logical_units.title: 11. Sale and substitution of repoed security
logical_units.text:
11. Sale and substitution of repoed security
(1) Securities purchased under repo may be-

logical_units.title: (a) On-sold either as an outright transaction or as part of another repo transaction.
logical_units.text:
(a) On-sold either as an outright transaction or as part of another repo transaction.
Outright sale of securities acquired under repo shall be undertaken only by such
entities that are eligible to undertake short sale transactions in terms of the relevant
directions of the Reserve Bank and in such securities that are permitted to be short
sold.

logical_units.title: (b) Substituted by another security in terms of the rules of any approved clearing
logical_units.text:
(b) Substituted by another security in terms of the rules of any approved clearing
agency.

logical_units.title: 12. Pricing of collateral, haircut and margining
logical_units.text:
12. Pricing of collateral, haircut and margining
(1) In case of repo transactions under these Directions-

logical_units.title: (a) Collaterals shall be priced transparently at prevailing market prices, in the first leg
logical_units.text:
(a) Collaterals shall be priced transparently at prevailing market prices, in the first leg
of a repo.

logical_units.title: (b) The price for the second leg will be the price for the first leg plus interest.
logical_units.text: (b) The price for the second leg will be the price for the first leg plus interest.

logical_units.title: (c) Haircut/ margins will be decided either by the clearing house or may be bilaterally
logical_units.text:
(c) Haircut/ margins will be decided either by the clearing house or may be bilaterally
agreed upon, in terms of the documentation governing repo transactions, subject to
the following stipulations:
i.
Listed corporate bonds and debentures shall carry a minimum haircut of 2% of
market value. Additional haircut may be charged based on tenor and illiquidity
of the security.
ii.
CPs and CDs shall carry a minimum haircut of 1.5% of market value.
iii.
Securities issued by a local authority shall carry a minimum haircut of 2% of
market value. Additional haircut may be charged based on tenor and illiquidity
of the security.

logical_units.title: 13. Accounting, presentation, valuation and disclosure
logical_units.text:
13. Accounting, presentation, valuation and disclosure
(1) Repos shall be accounted by entities regulated by the Reserve Bank as per
guidelines contained in Annex II.
(2) Other eligible participants may account for repo transactions as per applicable
accounting standards.

logical_units.title: 14. Computation of Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) /Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR)
logical_units.text:
14. Computation of Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) /Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR)
and borrowing limit
(1) Funds borrowed under repo including tri-party repo in government securities shall
be exempted from CRR/SLR computation and the security acquired under repo shall
be eligible for SLR provided the security is primarily eligible for SLR as per the
provisions of the Act under which it is required to be maintained.
(2) Borrowings by a bank through repo in corporate bonds and debentures shall be
reckoned as liabilities for Cash Reserve Ratio/ Statutory Liquidity Ratio requirement
and, to the extent these liabilities are to the banking system, they shall be netted as
per section 42(1) of the RBI Act, 1934.

logical_units.title: 15. Documentation
logical_units.text:
15. Documentation
(1) Participants shall enter into standard bilateral master repo agreements as per the
documentation finalized by FIMMDA.
(2) Repo transactions traded on a multilateral trading platform shall be governed by
the rules and regulations of the platform where it is traded.
(3) In case of tri-party repos, separate agreements between a participant and a tri-
party agent shall be executed as per the documentation prescribed by the tri-party
agent.

logical_units.title: 16. The list of previous circulars/directions issued by the Reserve Bank on repo
logical_units.text:
16. The list of previous circulars/directions issued by the Reserve Bank on repo
transaction that are repealed and withdrawn are given at Annex III hereunder.
(Dimple Bhandia)
Chief General Manager

logical_units.text:
